title: The code executes blockhashes (EIP-2935) at line 58 before beacon roots (EIP-4788) at line 59, violating the required order beacon_roots < history_storage. EthBlockExecutor mirrors this incorrect order at lines 106-108.
description_excerpt: Blocks processed with this implementation will have system calls in wrong order, causing consensus failures with clients following the correct specification order (beacon roots before history storage).

title: Proof fails at line 124: next_block_excess_blob_gas_osaka() receives input.base_fee_per_gas (child's base fee from parameter) instead of header.base_fee_per_gas() (parent's base fee). Inconsistent with using parent's excess_blob_gas (L122) and blob_g
description_excerpt: If EIP-7918 (Osaka) blob gas calculation depends on base fee (e.g., for dynamic blob pricing), using child's pre-computed base fee instead of parent's base fee could cause consensus splits when validators compute different next-block blob parameters, or enable blob fee manipulation by proposers.

title: Block executor validates regular gas limit (L119-127) but completely omits blob gas limit validation. Blob gas is accumulated (L152-154) without any check against MAX_BLOB_GAS_PER_BLOCK, allowing blocks to exceed consensus-mandated blob gas limits.
description_excerpt: Attacker submits multiple blob transactions whose cumulative blob gas exceeds MAX_BLOB_GAS_PER_BLOCK. Block executor accepts all transactions, producing an invalid block that violates consensus rules and will be rejected by other nodes.

title: Proof fails: validate_kzg_g1 (bytes.c:90) explicitly accepts point-at-infinity commitments without rejection. No additional infinity check exists before line 910 aggregation. Point-at-infinity contributes zero to weighted sum but corresponding proof
description_excerpt: Attacker sends P2P data availability sample with commitment=0xc00000...(infinity) plus arbitrary cell data and proof. Batch verification accepts the infinity commitment (bytes.c:90), aggregates it into random linear combination where it contributes zero, while weighted proof remains. Invalid cell data passes verification because commitment term vanishes from pairing equation.

title: The C function does not validate that input arrays (commitments_bytes, cell_indices, cells, proofs_bytes) actually have num_cells elements. Lines 838-840, 858, and 894-896 index/copy arrays assuming this length, causing out-of-bounds reads if a calle
description_excerpt: A C/C++ Ethereum client or code using FFI calls verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch with num_cells larger than actual array lengths. The function reads out-of-bounds memory at lines 838-840 (cell_indices), 858 (commitments_bytes memcpy), and 894-896 (proofs_bytes), potentially using garbage data in cryptographic verification, causing incorrect acceptance/rejection of proofs, process crash, or memory discl

title: Challenge hash uses first num_commitments elements from original commitments_bytes array instead of deduplicated unique_commitments array. When duplicate commitments exist (e.g., [A,B,A,C]), hash incorrectly includes [A,B,A] instead of [A,B,C], allow
description_excerpt: Attacker provides cells with commitments [A,B,A,C] vs [A,B,C,A]. Both deduplicate to [A,B,C] for verification, but hash different prefixes ([A,B,A] vs [A,B,C]), producing different Fiat-Shamir challenges despite identical cryptographic content, enabling selective forgery by searching for favorable challenge values.

title: The recovered polynomial coefficients are not validated to have degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB. While the encoding side (eip7594.c:94-96) asserts the upper half coefficients are zero, recover_cells computes a full FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB coeffi
description_excerpt: An attacker crafts malicious cells that produce a recovered polynomial with non-zero coefficients in the upper half. When recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs recomputes proofs (eip7594.c:264), compute_fk20_cell_proofs expects only FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB coefficients but receives incorrect data, producing invalid proofs that could bypass downstream validation or cause state inconsistencies.

title: Challenge computation at line 877 passes commitments_bytes (original array with duplicates) instead of unique_commitments (deduplicated array) to compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge. This causes the challenge hash to include the first num_c
description_excerpt: Attacker crafts two P2P messages with identical unique commitments but different orderings (e.g., [C_A, C_A, C_B] vs [C_B, C_A, C_A]). The challenge function hashes different commitment sequences, generating different challenge values r1 ≠ r2 for semantically equivalent verification contexts, potentially accepting invalid proofs.

title: Inclusion proof verification is skipped for ExecutionLayer origin (line 2185), allowing compromised/buggy EL to send data column sidecars with invalid inclusion proofs. Blob sidecars verify inclusion proofs unconditionally (line 1956), proving this s
description_excerpt: Compromised execution layer sends data column sidecar with kzg_commitments that don't match block body_root. CL accepts it because inclusion proof check is skipped for EL origin, potentially serving invalid data to peers or causing consensus divergence.

title: JWT authentication is optional: auth.headers() returns Ok(None) when no jwt_secret is configured (auth.rs:96-98), yet Engine API calls proceed without authentication. Blobs from unauthenticated EL connections are marked ExecutionLayer origin and skip
description_excerpt: Operator starts Grandine without --jwt-secret flag. Attacker performs MITM or DNS poisoning on localhost EL connection, serves malicious blobs via engine_getBlobsV1. Blobs skip cryptographic verification and are accepted into fork choice.

title: Column-level 50% threshold check (≥64/128 columns) does not guarantee per-blob 50% threshold. When columns contain different subsets of blobs, individual blobs may have <50% cells available even when total column count is sufficient, causing KZG reco
description_excerpt: Attacker provides 64 columns where 40 columns contain blob A cells but only 32 contain blob B cells. Blob A recovers successfully, but blob B has only 25% cells (32/128), violating erasure coding threshold and potentially producing invalid recovered data.

title: Line 148 uses blob_entry.epoch (the schedule entry's activation epoch) instead of the current epoch parameter when computing the blob params hash. This makes fork digest constant across all epochs within the same blob schedule period, violating the e
description_excerpt: Attacker deploys nodes with blob schedule configured at different activation epochs (e.g., epoch 100 vs 200) but same max_blobs value. At epoch 300, both node sets are in identical state but compute different fork digests (hash(100||15) vs hash(200||15)). This causes P2P topic subscription mismatch and network partition between otherwise-compatible nodes.

title: The clamping logic count.max(validator_custody_requirement).min(number_of_custody_groups) only enforces the invariant when validator_custody_requirement <= number_of_custody_groups, but Config has no validation of this constraint (types/src/config.rs
description_excerpt: A malicious testnet operator sets validator_custody_requirement=200 and number_of_custody_groups=100 in the network config. Validators with moderate stake (count between 100-200) will be assigned custody requirement of 100 instead of the mandated 200, causing them to subscribe to fewer custody groups than required, potentially breaking PeerDAS data availability guarantees.

title: KZG proof verification returns Result<bool>, but line 2195-2199 uses .map_err()? which only handles errors, discarding the boolean result. When verify_kzg_proofs returns Ok(false) (invalid proofs), execution continues and the sidecar is accepted at l
description_excerpt: Attacker sends P2P data column sidecar with invalid KZG proofs. The gossip validation at validate_data_column_sidecar_with_state calls verify_kzg_proofs which returns Ok(false), but this boolean is discarded by .map_err()?. The sidecar passes all checks and is accepted, allowing propagation of invalid erasure-coded data across the network.

title: The handler rejects out-of-range requests with RpcErrorResponse::InvalidRequest (error code 1) at L1402-1408, but the Ethereum consensus spec mandates RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE (error code 3) for requests outside the data_column_serve_range. InvalidReques
description_excerpt: A peer requesting data columns outside the serve range receives InvalidRequest instead of ResourceUnavailable, violating spec compliance and potentially causing interoperability issues where spec-compliant peers may ban or deprioritize this node for incorrect error responses.

title: Cache prunes data columns after only 2 slots (DATA_COLUMN_RETAIN_DURATION_IN_SLOTS=2) instead of the required 131,072 slots (MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS=4096 epochs). This 65,536x discrepancy causes premature eviction of data that mu
description_excerpt: Peer requests data columns for slot current_slot-100 (valid per MIN_EPOCHS). P2P handler validates request as legitimate, but cache has already pruned entries older than 2 slots. Node fails to serve valid historical data, violating protocol availability requirements.

title: The property holds IF BPO forks are correctly tracked in ForkContext.next_fork() separate from phase transitions. Lines 655 and 661-664 correctly keep next_fork_version=current and set next_fork_epoch from fork_context. However, ForkContext is extern
description_excerpt: If ForkContext fails to track BPO forks separately, nodes would advertise next_fork_epoch=FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH in their ENR, causing peer discovery issues and potential network splits when BPO forks activate.

title: Missing validation: data column sidecars do not check kzg_commitments.len() <= max_blobs_per_block(epoch). Blob sidecars enforce this at L1838-1846, but the equivalent check is absent from data column validation.
description_excerpt: Attacker sends data column sidecar via P2P gossip with kzg_commitments.len() exceeding epoch's max_blobs_per_block (e.g., 100 blobs when limit is 21), causing resource exhaustion during KZG verification and violating consensus protocol blob limits.

title: Config::validate() only checks config_name, never validates fork epoch ordering. Config::phase_at_epoch() (L920-926) relies on take_while over ordered epochs; misordered epochs cause wrong phase detection. Custom YAML configs can supply any epoch val
description_excerpt: Operator loads malicious config YAML with misordered epochs (e.g., electra_fork_epoch < deneb_fork_epoch). Node accepts config after validate(). phase_at_epoch() returns wrong phases, causing phase-specific consensus rules to be misapplied or skipped.

title: Selector assumes descending order and returns first match via find_map. Named network constructors (sepolia L512-520, holesky L637-645, hoodi L739-747) define blob_schedule in ascending order, bypassing serde sorting. With ascending data, first match
description_excerpt: On Sepolia at epoch 280000, selector returns entry (274176, max_blobs=15) instead of correct entry (275712, max_blobs=21). Node rejects valid blocks with 16-21 blobs, causing consensus failure.

title: Validation exists but is only enforced on gossip path (L480). RPC paths (DataColumnsByRoot/ByRange at lighthouse_network/src/rpc/codec.rs:696,717) and database loads (store/src/hot_cold_store.rs:2529,3252) bypass the guard, allowing mismatched array
description_excerpt: Malicious peer sends RPC response with column.len()≠kzg_commitments.len()≠kzg_proofs.len(). Deserialization succeeds, validation is skipped, KZG verification accesses wrong indices causing out-of-bounds panic or incorrect proof validation.
keyword_matched: KZG/blob+commitment; P2P/gossip+validation; state+transition/SSZ; JWT/auth+bypass; memory+safety/bounds; inclusion+proof/merkle

title: The request validation only limits the number of identifiers to max_request_blocks_deneb (128) at decode time in codec.rs:562-570, but does not validate the total column count. An attacker can send 128 identifiers each with 128 columns for 16384 tota
description_excerpt: Attacker sends DataColumnsByRoot request with 128 identifiers, each containing max columns per identifier. Total columns requested = 128 * number_of_columns() which can exceed 16384. Node processes all columns, consuming excessive memory and CPU for reconstruction/validation, enabling resource exhaustion DoS.

title: NoPeer error from retry_columns_by_range is caught at L1102 but only logged, not propagated. Function returns Ok(KeepChain) at L1107, leaving batch in Downloading state permanently. Batch never transitions to Failed or AwaitingDownload, preventing re
description_excerpt: Attacker controls all custody peers for a column or network partitions the node from custody peers. Partial batch retry fails with NoPeer, error is silently swallowed, sync stalls permanently for that batch. Node cannot complete chain sync.

title: Handler lacks epoch-based deprecation check. After FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, the handler continues processing BlobsByRoot requests normally instead of rejecting them. No validation compares current_epoch against fulu_fo
description_excerpt: After deprecation threshold (FULU_FORK_EPOCH + 4096 epochs), any peer sends BlobsByRoot RPC request. Node processes it normally via handle_blobs_by_root_request_inner, responding with blobs if available, violating protocol spec deprecation requirement. Causes protocol non-compliance and resource waste on deprecated endpoints.

description_excerpt: Malicious EL provides invalid KZG proofs via engine_getBlobsV2. Consensus layer accepts and caches them without verification (from_execution_verified), then propagates them on gossip, bypassing KZG checks for cached columns. Network accepts invalid data availability proofs.

title: Post-Fulu spec requires get_beacon_proposer_index to return proposer_lookahead[slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH]. All three gossip verification paths (data_column L688, blob L536, block L991) call get_beacon_proposer_indices instead, which always recomputes fr
description_excerpt: If proposer_lookahead and recomputed values diverge due to state inconsistencies in cheap_state_advance partial updates, different nodes may accept/reject the same block based on which code path executes, causing consensus splits.

title: The empty kzg_commitments check at L538-540 is enforced only in gossip and block publishing paths. RPC-received data columns bypass verify_data_column_sidecar entirely, going directly to KZG verification in data_availability_checker.rs:446, which acc
description_excerpt: Attacker sends DataColumnSidecar with empty kzg_commitments via RPC DataColumnsByRange/DataColumnsByRoot request. Bypasses structural validation, passes KZG batch verification (empty list valid), and gets imported into availability cache with semantically invalid state.

title: Columns from EL (engine_getBlobs) bypass gossip verification via new_for_block_publishing, which skips signature, parent, inclusion proof, and KZG verification, yet are treated as gossip-verified when passed to process_gossip_data_columns. Malicious
description_excerpt: Compromised local EL returns crafted columns via engine_getBlobs with invalid signatures/proofs. These bypass gossip checks via new_for_block_publishing (L221-250), get published to network via publish_column_sidecars, and propagated as valid. Network peers reject them after full verification, causing failed block import and potential slashing.
keyword_matched: signature+replay/validation; consensus+fork/split; KZG/blob+commitment; P2P/gossip+validation; JWT/auth+bypass; inclusion+proof/merkle

title: publish_column_sidecars() publishes data columns to all computed subnets without validating against the node's sampling_subnets. The subnet is derived from column index (L525) and published directly (L529) with no check that subnet ∈ network_globals.
description_excerpt: A block producer publishes all 128 data columns. The node publishes to all 128 subnets even though it's only subscribed to ~8 custody subnets, violating the PeerDAS spec and enabling network pollution/DOS through unsubscribed subnet publishing.

title: Blobs from EL are wrapped with KzgVerifiedBlob::from_execution_verified() which skips KZG proof verification entirely. Same issue in v2 path where blobs_to_data_column_sidecars uses EL-provided proofs without validation before propagation.
description_excerpt: Malicious or compromised EL sends invalid blob-commitment pairs. CL accepts them without verification, propagates to network, causing consensus splits when honest nodes reject invalid blobs.

title: Data columns are added to cache during KZG verification (block_verification.rs:1324-1332) BEFORE state transition validation (per_block_processing at L1614-1629). When blocks fail state transition, their columns remain in cache. RPC handler serves ca
description_excerpt: Attacker sends invalid block via RPC; KZG verification caches its data columns before state transition runs; block fails per_block_processing and is rejected; columns remain cached; honest peer requests DataColumnsByRoot and receives columns for the invalid block.

title: When blob_retention_epoch > fulu_fork_epoch, line 829 returns current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS without enforcing max(result, FULU_FORK_EPOCH). During the window where current_epoch < FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_C
description_excerpt: Within 4096 epochs after Fulu fork (when blob_retention_epoch > fulu_fork_epoch but current_epoch - 4096 < fulu_fork_epoch), an attacker can send DataColumnsByRangeRequest for slots before FULU_FORK_EPOCH, requesting data that should not exist (pre-fork). This causes nodes to accept requests for invalid epoch ranges, potentially causing DoS or inconsistent sync state.

title: BlobSchedule deserialization and construction lack validation that max_blobs_per_block <= MAX_BLOB_COMMITMENTS_PER_BLOCK (4096). Config entries can specify arbitrary values (e.g., 5000) which are accepted without bounds checking, creating type-safety

Nodes load config successfully, then accept blocks with 4500 blob commitments (passing runtime check at block_verification.rs:885) but violating SSZ VariableList constraints, causing divergent validation behavior.

title: RPC-imported columns bypass observed_column_sidecars cache. Gossip path calls observe_gossip_data_column (L521 data_column_verification.rs) which updates the cache, but put_rpc_custody_columns only updates observed_slashable. A proposer can publish c

Attacker then serves conflicting column C2 (same index, different data, same signature) via RPC to specific peers. RPC path imports C2 without checking observed_column_sidecars, allowing two contradictory columns for the same (slot, proposer, index) tuple to coexist across the netwo

description_excerpt: Attacker responds to DataColumnsByRange with columns having valid KZG proofs but forged signatures or invalid merkle proofs. Columns pass KZG check (L446), get marked available (L467-475), and could be reconstructed or propagated without detecting the forgery.

title: BlobsByRange protocol remains in supported_protocols list forever (conditional only on Deneb fork existence, not epoch). No code rejects requests after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS. Handler only validates availability boundary for pruning, not deprec
description_excerpt: After FULU activation + MIN_EPOCHS, attacker sends BlobsByRange requests to force nodes to serve deprecated blob data instead of data columns, bypassing PeerDAS migration and enabling resource exhaustion via dual serving obligations.

description_excerpt: Local block construction bug or external data source provides sidecar with slot <= parent.slot. Call new_for_block_publishing, which accepts it without slot validation. Result is GossipVerifiedDataColumn that propagates invalid data over network, wasting bandwidth and triggering peer penalties.

description_excerpt: Peer requests BlobsByRange for slots >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH. Node serves post-Fulu blobs via deprecated RPC method instead of rejecting or filtering, causing protocol confusion and potential consensus divergence with clients that correctly implement the fork transition.

title: IGNORE validation prevents forwarding (correct) but lacks explicit re-processing queue. Messages with FutureSlot/PastFinalizedSlot errors return IGNORE without application-level re-queuing, unlike ParentUnknown which explicitly queues for sync. Proof

description_excerpt: Peer requests blobs from early FULU_FORK_EPOCH slots while those blobs are unavailable (pruned or never stored post-PeerDAS activation). If oldest_blob_slot > request slot but within data_availability_boundary, peer receives LowToleranceError penalty despite making a legitimate transition-period request.

Downstream KZG operations using cell_ids.push(data_column.index) at line 396 of reconstruct_data_columns will use wrong column indices for cryptographic verification, potentially causing incorrect reconstruction or bypassing data availability guarantees.

description_excerpt: Malicious peer sends DataColumnsByRoot responses with index >= 128. Node performs costly KZG verification before filtering. Attacker repeats to exhaust CPU resources. No peer penalty applied since validation succeeds but columns are silently dropped.

description_excerpt: Operator loads custom config with deneb_fork_epoch < capella_fork_epoch. At intermediate epochs, node applies wrong fork rules (e.g., Deneb logic instead of Capella), diverging from honest peers and potentially accepting invalid blocks.

Both sidecars pass validation and are cached separately (different blockRootHex values), violating spec requirement to accept only the first sidecar for tuple (slot, proposer_index, index).

description_excerpt: Malicious proposer sends two DataColumnSidecars for (slot=N, proposer=V, index=I) with different content and valid signatures. Both are accepted and stored under different blockRootHex keys, enabling equivocation attacks.

description_excerpt: Attacker sends DataColumnSidecarsByRangeRequest with startSlot from epochs before max(current_epoch - 4096, FULU_FORK_EPOCH), bypassing validation. Node wastes resources attempting to serve data columns from epochs it's not required to retain, or serves incorrect partial data.

TxPool accepts it (old spec, cap=MaxValue), peers broadcast it, but block producers reject it when building block N (new spec, cap=16,777,216). This creates mempool pollution and wastes network bandwidth on un-mineable transactions.

Transactions are accepted into mempool, broadcast to network, consume resources until next head change triggers eviction. Repeated submissions cause sustained mempool pollution.

description_excerpt: Attacker sends blob tx with more BlobVersionedHashes than wrapper.Blobs. Extra hashes pass format check in first loop but skip commitment validation in second loop, breaking integrity guarantee that every hash commits to an actual blob.

description_excerpt: Attacker constructs block with transactions exceeding EIP-7825 gas cap and propagates via P2P. BlockchainProcessor enqueues block, BranchProcessor calls BlockProcessor.ProcessOne which executes all transactions before any validation, consuming excessive gas and enabling DoS.
